findings.message: New Dashboard view fetches /internal/summary with no error instrumentation: the fetch().then().then(setData) chain has no .catch and the component has no error boundary. A failed request (network error or non-ok response left unguarded) is silently swallowed, so this new user-visible code path is completely unobserved by RUM. The window error / unhandledrejection handlers in rum.ts will not reliably capture a rejection that React/await semantics may consume, and there is no js-errors signal tied to this view's failure mode.
findings.suggestion: Add a .catch that surfaces the failure to the RUM error path (e.g. record a js-error / push a {kind:"error"} RUM event or rethrow so window.onunhandledrejection fires), and wrap the view in an error boundary so render/fetch failures produce an observable signal instead of a blank section.
